tasks.name: Create Database Migration for Bookmarks RLS Policies
tasks.description: Create a migration file to add Row Level Security (RLS) policies to the existing bookmarks table. This will ensure that only researchers with active subscriptions can create bookmarks, and users can only view and delete their own bookmarks.

relatedFiles.description: Reference for similar RLS policies implementation

tasks.implementationGuide:
Create a new migration file in the supabase/migrations directory with the following policies:
1. Enable RLS on the bookmarks table
2. Policy for users to view their own bookmarks
3. Policy for researchers to create bookmarks (with subscription check)
4. Policy for users to delete their own bookmarks

Follow the pattern in existing migration files like 20250509171204_create_researcher_topic_subscriptions.sql
tasks.verificationCriteria: The migration file correctly defines all necessary RLS policies for the bookmarks table. The policies should enable researchers with active subscriptions to create and delete bookmarks, and limit users to only see their own bookmarks.
tasks.analysisResult:
Based on the project analysis, we're implementing a bookmarking feature for Eventy360 with these key components:

1. Database: Using the existing bookmarks table with new RLS policies
2. Server Actions: For toggling bookmarks and retrieving bookmarked events
3. UI Components: BookmarkButton component and updates to EventCardGrid
4. Pages: New bookmarks page in the profile section
5. Navigation: New sidebar item for bookmarks (researchers only)
6. Security: Limiting functionality to researchers with active subscriptions
7. Internationalization: Support for Arabic (RTL) and English

The implementation follows existing project patterns for components, server actions, security, and i18n support.
tasks.summary: Successfully created a migration file that adds Row Level Security policies to the bookmarks table, following existing project patterns. The policies ensure that only researchers with active or trial subscriptions can create and update bookmarks, while all users can view and delete their own bookmarks.

tasks.name: Create Bookmark Server Actions
tasks.description: Implement server actions for toggling bookmarks and retrieving bookmarked events. These actions will handle creating and removing bookmarks and include subscription checks.

relatedFiles.description: New server actions file for bookmarks functionality

tasks.implementationGuide:
Create src/app/[locale]/profile/bookmarks/actions.ts with:
1. toggleBookmark(eventId) function to add/remove a bookmark
2. getBookmarkedEvents(locale) function to retrieve bookmarked events
3. Subscription checks for researchers using existing patterns
4. Proper error handling and response format

Follow the pattern in src/app/[locale]/profile/submissions/actions.ts
tasks.verificationCriteria: Server actions successfully handle bookmark operations with proper error handling, subscription checks, and security. The actions should integrate with the existing Supabase client and follow project patterns.
tasks.analysisResult:
Based on the project analysis, we're implementing a bookmarking feature for Eventy360 with these key components:

1. Database: Using the existing bookmarks table with new RLS policies
2. Server Actions: For toggling bookmarks and retrieving bookmarked events
3. UI Components: BookmarkButton component and updates to EventCardGrid
4. Pages: New bookmarks page in the profile section
5. Navigation: New sidebar item for bookmarks (researchers only)
6. Security: Limiting functionality to researchers with active subscriptions
7. Internationalization: Support for Arabic (RTL) and English

The implementation follows existing project patterns for components, server actions, security, and i18n support.
tasks.summary: Successfully implemented three server actions for bookmarking functionality: toggleBookmark to add/remove bookmarks, isEventBookmarked to check bookmark status, and getBookmarkedEvents to retrieve all bookmarked events. The implementation includes proper error handling, authentication checks, and internationalization support.

tasks.name: Create BookmarkButton Component
tasks.description: Create a reusable BookmarkButton component that can be used in event cards and event details pages to toggle bookmarks with visual feedback.
tasks.implementationGuide:
Create src/components/events/common/BookmarkButton.tsx:
1. Implement states: bookmarked, unbookmarked, loading
2. Call toggleBookmark action on click
3. Show tooltip for different states
4. Support RTL layout for Arabic
5. Implement subscription check to disable for non-subscribers

Use existing UI component patterns from Flowbite
tasks.verificationCriteria: BookmarkButton component correctly shows different visual states, handles click events, and includes subscription checks. It should support RTL layout and include proper accessibility attributes.
tasks.analysisResult:
Based on the project analysis, we're implementing a bookmarking feature for Eventy360 with these key components:

2. Server Actions: For toggling bookmarks and retrieving bookmarked events
3. UI Components: BookmarkButton component and updates to EventCardGrid
4. Pages: New bookmarks page in the profile section
5. Navigation: New sidebar item for bookmarks (researchers only)
6. Security: Limiting functionality to researchers with active subscriptions
7. Internationalization: Support for Arabic (RTL) and English

The implementation follows existing project patterns for components, server actions, security, and i18n support.
tasks.summary: Successfully implemented the BookmarkButton component with all required features: bookmark toggle functionality, loading/bookmarked/unbookmarked states, tooltip support, subscription check, and internationalization support. Added Arabic translations for the BookmarkButton component and navigation.

tasks.name: Update EventCardGrid Component
tasks.description: Integrate the BookmarkButton component into the EventCardGrid component to enable bookmarking functionality in event cards.
tasks.implementationGuide:
Update src/components/events/discovery/EventCardGrid.tsx to:
1. Import the BookmarkButton component
2. Replace the disabled bookmark button with BookmarkButton
3. Pass necessary props (eventId, locale)
4. Handle isBookmarked state
tasks.verificationCriteria: BookmarkButton is correctly integrated into the EventCardGrid component. Event cards in the discovery grid allow users to bookmark and unbookmark events.
tasks.analysisResult:

tasks.summary: Successfully integrated BookmarkButton into EventCardGrid component. Used useEffect to check bookmark status on mount, added state management for bookmarked status, removed the static bookmark button, and properly passed all necessary props to BookmarkButton including initialBookmarked state.

tasks.name: Create Bookmarks Page
tasks.description: Create a new page to display all bookmarked events for the logged-in researcher using the existing EventCardGrid component.
tasks.implementationGuide:
Create src/app/[locale]/profile/bookmarks/page.tsx with:
1. Use ProfilePageHeader for consistent UI
2. Import getBookmarkedEvents from actions.ts
3. Fetch bookmarked events with proper loading states
4. Use EventCardGrid to display bookmarked events
5. Add empty state for no bookmarks

Follow the pattern in other profile pages
tasks.verificationCriteria: Bookmarks page correctly displays all bookmarked events using the EventCardGrid component, with proper loading states and empty state handling.

Added proper authentication checks, researcher role verification, and internationalization support. Used ProfilePageHeader and ProfileCard components for consistent UI. Added missing translations to support all necessary strings in the UI.

tasks.name: Update Profile Sidebar

3. Add the new route to the bookmarks page

Follow the pattern of other navigation items in the sidebar
tasks.verificationCriteria: A new sidebar item for bookmarks is correctly added to the profile sidebar for researchers only. The item should navigate to the bookmarks page when clicked.
